006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.cbor;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.math.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
026
027/**
028 * Specialized output stream for serializing CBOR streams (RFC 8949).
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * CBOR uses a self-describing binary format where each data item starts with an initial byte
032 * containing a 3-bit major type and 5-bit additional information. Integers use compact encoding;
033 * strings, arrays, and maps use definite-length encoding.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
036 *    <li class='note'>This class is not intended for external use.
037 *    <li class='note'>This class is not thread-safe; each serialization session uses its own instance.
038 * </ul>
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
041 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Cbor">CBOR Basics</a>
042 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8949.html">RFC 8949</a>
043 * </ul>
044 */
045@SuppressWarnings({
046   "resource" // appendXxx(...) methods return this stream for chaining; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
047})
048public class CborOutputStream extends OutputStream {
049
050   private final OutputStream os;
051
052   /**
053    * Constructor.
054    *
055    * @param os The output stream being wrapped.
056    */
057   protected CborOutputStream(OutputStream os) {
058      this.os = os;
059   }
060
061   @Override /* Overridden from OutputStream */
062   public void write(int b) {
063      try {
064         os.write(b);
065      } catch (IOException e) {
066         throw new SerializeException(e);
067      }
068   }
069
070   @Override /* Overridden from OutputStream */
071   public void write(byte[] b, int off, int len) {
072      try {
073         os.write(b, off, len);
074      } catch (IOException e) {
075         throw new SerializeException(e);
076      }
077   }
078
079   /**
080    * Writes a single byte to the stream.
081    *
082    * @param b The byte to write (lower 8 bits used).
083    */
084   void write1(int b) {
085      try {
086         os.write(b & 0xFF);
087      } catch (IOException e) {
088         throw new SerializeException(e);
089      }
090   }
091
092   /**
093    * Writes a 2-byte big-endian unsigned integer.
094    *
095    * @param value The value to write.
096    */
097   void write2(int value) {
098      write1(value >> 8);
099      write1(value);
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Writes a 4-byte big-endian unsigned integer.
104    *
105    * @param value The value to write.
106    */
107   void write4(int value) {
108      write1(value >> 24);
109      write1(value >> 16);
110      write1(value >> 8);
111      write1(value);
112   }
113
114   /**
115    * Writes an 8-byte big-endian unsigned integer.
116    *
117    * @param value The value to write.
118    */
119   void write8(long value) {
120      write1((int)(value >> 56));
121      write1((int)(value >> 48));
122      write1((int)(value >> 40));
123      write1((int)(value >> 32));
124      write1((int)(value >> 24));
125      write1((int)(value >> 16));
126      write1((int)(value >> 8));
127      write1((int)(value));
128   }
129
130   /**
131    * Encodes and writes a CBOR initial byte with major type and argument.
132    *
133    * <p>
134    * Argument encoding per RFC 8949:
135    * <ul>
136    *    <li>0-23: Single byte (majorType &lt;&lt; 5 | argument)
137    *    <li>24-255: 2 bytes (type | 24, 1-byte argument)
138    *    <li>256-65535: 3 bytes (type | 25, 2-byte argument)
139    *    <li>65536-2^32-1: 5 bytes (type | 26, 4-byte argument)
140    *    <li>2^32 and above: 9 bytes (type | 27, 8-byte argument)
141    * </ul>
142    *
143    * @param majorType The CBOR major type (0-7).
144    * @param argument The additional information or length value.
145    */
146   void writeHead(int majorType, long argument) {
147      int typeBits = (majorType & 0x07) << 5;
148      if (argument <= 23)
149         write1(typeBits | (int)argument);
150      else if (argument <= 0xFF) {
151         write1(typeBits | 24);
152         write1((int)argument);
153      } else if (argument <= 0xFFFF) {
154         write1(typeBits | 25);
155         write2((int)argument);
156      } else if (argument <= 0xFFFFFFFFL) {
157         write1(typeBits | 26);
158         write4((int)argument);
159      } else {
160         write1(typeBits | 27);
161         write8(argument);
162      }
163   }
164
165   /**
166    * Appends CBOR null (0xF6).
167    *
168    * @return This stream.
169    */
170   CborOutputStream appendNull() {
171      write1(0xF6);
172      return this;
173   }
174
175   /**
176    * Appends CBOR undefined (0xF7).
177    *
178    * @return This stream.
179    */
180   CborOutputStream appendUndefined() {
181      write1(0xF7);
182      return this;
183   }
184
185   /**
186    * Appends a boolean value (0xF4 for false, 0xF5 for true).
187    *
188    * @param value The boolean value.
189    * @return This stream.
190    */
191   CborOutputStream appendBoolean(boolean value) {
192      write1(value ? 0xF5 : 0xF4);
193      return this;
194   }
195
196   /**
197    * Appends an integer using CBOR compact encoding.
198    * Positive values use major type 0; negative values use major type 1 with argument -1-value.
199    *
200    * @param value The integer value.
201    * @return This stream.
202    */
203   CborOutputStream appendInt(int value) {
204      if (value >= 0)
205         writeHead(0, value & 0xFFFFFFFFL);
206      else
207         writeHead(1, -1L - value);
208      return this;
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * Appends a long integer using CBOR compact encoding.
213    *
214    * @param value The long value.
215    * @return This stream.
216    */
217   CborOutputStream appendLong(long value) {
218      if (value >= 0)
219         writeHead(0, value);
220      else
221         writeHead(1, -1L - value);
222      return this;
223   }
224
225   /**
226    * Appends a float as IEEE 754 single-precision (0xFA + 4 bytes).
227    *
228    * @param value The float value.
229    * @return This stream.
230    */
231   CborOutputStream appendFloat(float value) {
232      write1(0xFA);
233      write4(Float.floatToRawIntBits(value));
234      return this;
235   }
236
237   /**
238    * Appends a double as IEEE 754 double-precision (0xFB + 8 bytes).
239    *
240    * @param value The double value.
241    * @return This stream.
242    */
243   CborOutputStream appendDouble(double value) {
244      write1(0xFB);
245      write8(Double.doubleToRawLongBits(value));
246      return this;
247   }
248
249   /**
250    * Appends a generic Number, dispatching to the appropriate append method.
251    *
252    * @param n The number.
253    * @return This stream.
254    */
255   CborOutputStream appendNumber(Number n) {
256      var c = n.getClass();
257      if (c == Integer.class || c == Short.class || c == Byte.class || c == AtomicInteger.class)
258         return appendInt(n.intValue());
259      if (c == Long.class || c == AtomicLong.class)
260         return appendLong(n.longValue());
261      if (c == Float.class)
262         return appendFloat(n.floatValue());
263      if (c == Double.class)
264         return appendDouble(n.doubleValue());
265      if (c == BigInteger.class)
266         return appendBigInteger((BigInteger)n);
267      if (c == BigDecimal.class)
268         return appendString(n.toString());
269      return appendInt(0);
270   }
271
272   /**
273    * Appends a {@link BigInteger} losslessly.
274    *
275    * <p>
276    * Values within the signed {@code long} range use the compact integer encoding.  Values that
277    * still fit CBOR's native integer range (unsigned 64-bit for positives via major type 0, down to
278    * {@code -2^64} for negatives via major type 1) are emitted with the full 64-bit magnitude rather
279    * than truncating.  Values beyond {@code ±2^64} (which have no native CBOR integer head) fall back
280    * to a lossless decimal string so no magnitude is lost.
281    *
282    * @param value The value.
283    * @return This stream.
284    */
285   CborOutputStream appendBigInteger(BigInteger value) {
286      if (value.bitLength() <= 63)
287         return appendLong(value.longValueExact());
288      if (value.signum() > 0) {
289         if (value.bitLength() <= 64) {
290            write1(0x1B);  // major type 0 | additional info 27 (8-byte argument)
291            write8(value.longValue());  // low 64 bits == full unsigned magnitude here
292            return this;
293         }
294      } else {
295         var arg = value.negate().subtract(BigInteger.ONE);  // CBOR major type 1 argument == -1 - value
296         if (arg.bitLength() <= 64) {
297            write1(0x3B);  // major type 1 | additional info 27 (8-byte argument)
298            write8(arg.longValue());
299            return this;
300         }
301      }
302      // Beyond ±2^64: no native CBOR integer head exists; emit a lossless decimal string.
303      return appendString(value.toString());
304   }
305
306   @SuppressWarnings({
307      "java:S127" // Loop counter advances for surrogate pairs
308   })
309   private static int getUtf8ByteLength(CharSequence cs) {
310      var count = 0;
311      for (int i = 0, len = cs.length(); i < len; i++) {
312         var ch = cs.charAt(i);
313         if (ch <= 0x7F)
314            count++;
315         else if (ch <= 0x7FF)
316            count += 2;
317         else if (Character.isHighSurrogate(ch) && i + 1 < len && Character.isLowSurrogate(cs.charAt(i + 1))) {
318            count += 4;
319            ++i;
320         } else
321            // BMP char, or an unpaired surrogate which is emitted as the 3-byte U+FFFD replacement.
322            count += 3;
323      }
324      return count;
325   }
326
327   @SuppressWarnings({
328      "java:S127"  // For-loop counter modification acceptable in this algorithm
329   })
330   private int writeUtf8To(CharSequence in) {
331      var count = 0;
332      for (int i = 0, len = in.length(); i < len; i++) {
333         var c = (in.charAt(i) & 0xFFFF);
334         if (c <= 0x7F) {
335            write((byte)(c & 0xFF));
336            count++;
337         } else if (c <= 0x7FF) {
338            write((byte)(0xC0 + ((c >> 6) & 0x1F)));
339            write((byte)(0x80 + (c & 0x3F)));
340            count += 2;
341         } else if (c >= 0xD800 && c <= 0xDBFF && i + 1 < len && Character.isLowSurrogate(in.charAt(i + 1))) {
342            int jchar2 = in.charAt(++i) & 0xFFFF;
343            int n = (c << 10) + jchar2 + 0xFCA02400;
344            write((byte)(0xF0 + ((n >> 18) & 0x07)));
345            write((byte)(0x80 + ((n >> 12) & 0x3F)));
346            write((byte)(0x80 + ((n >> 6) & 0x3F)));
347            write((byte)(0x80 + (n & 0x3F)));
348            count += 4;
349         } else {
350            // BMP char, or an unpaired surrogate.  An unpaired surrogate is not a valid Unicode
351            // scalar value, so emit the U+FFFD replacement character rather than crashing or
352            // producing malformed UTF-8.
353            if (c >= 0xD800 && c <= 0xDFFF)
354               c = 0xFFFD;
355            write((byte)(0xE0 + ((c >> 12) & 0x0F)));
356            write((byte)(0x80 + ((c >> 6) & 0x3F)));
357            write((byte)(0x80 + (c & 0x3F)));
358            count += 3;
359         }
360      }
361      return count;
362   }
363
364   /**
365    * Appends a UTF-8 text string (major type 3).
366    *
367    * @param s The string.
368    * @return This stream.
369    */
370   CborOutputStream appendString(CharSequence s) {
371      int length = getUtf8ByteLength(s);
372      writeHead(3, length);
373      int length2 = writeUtf8To(s);
374      if (length != length2)
375         throw new SerializeException("Unexpected length.  Expected={0}, Actual={1}", length, length2);
376      return this;
377   }
378
379   /**
380    * Appends a byte string (major type 2).
381    *
382    * @param data The bytes.
383    * @return This stream.
384    */
385   CborOutputStream appendBinary(byte[] data) {
386      writeHead(2, data.length);
387      try {
388         os.write(data);
389      } catch (IOException e) {
390         throw new SerializeException(e);
391      }
392      return this;
393   }
394
395   /**
396    * Appends a byte string from an input stream.
397    *
398    * @param data The input stream.
399    * @return This stream.
400    */
401   CborOutputStream appendBinary(InputStream data) {
402      var baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
403      pipe(data, baos, x -> {
404         throw new SerializeException(x);
405      });
406      return appendBinary(baos.toByteArray());
407   }
408
409   /**
410    * Starts a definite-length array (major type 4).
411    *
412    * @param size The number of elements.
413    * @return This stream.
414    */
415   CborOutputStream startArray(int size) {
416      writeHead(4, size);
417      return this;
418   }
419
420   /**
421    * Starts a definite-length map (major type 5).
422    *
423    * @param size The number of key-value pairs.
424    * @return This stream.
425    */
426   CborOutputStream startMap(int size) {
427      writeHead(5, size);
428      return this;
429   }
430
431   /**
432    * Writes a CBOR semantic tag (major type 6).
433    *
434    * @param tagNumber The tag number.
435    * @return This stream.
436    */
437   CborOutputStream writeTag(long tagNumber) {
438      writeHead(6, tagNumber);
439      return this;
440   }
441
442   /**
443    * Writes a CBOR simple value (major type 7).
444    *
445    * <p>
446    * Caller responsibility: reserved encodings (20-23 = bool/null/undefined; 25-27 =
447    * float16/32/64; 31 = break) collide with native scalar/structural emits and must not be used
448    * here.
449    *
450    * @param value The simple value (range {@code 0..255}; reserved values noted above).
451    * @return This stream.
452    */
453   CborOutputStream writeSimple(int value) {
454      writeHead(7, value);
455      return this;
456   }
457}
